source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Three point charges, of charge \( +Q \), \( -2Q \), and \( +3Q \), are placed equidistant as shown. Which vector best describes the net direction of the electric force acting on the \( +Q \) charge?
<image_1><image_2>
extraction_notes: options only provide letter-like labels rather than descriptive option text
image fields contain PIL image object representations, not explicit file paths or URLs

question_text contains two image placeholders (<image_1><image_2>) but only one explicit image path is provided in image_paths

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'equidistant as shown', indicating the spatial arrangement in the image is necessary
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false both point to strong visual dependence
the task asks for the net direction vector on the +Q charge, which depends on geometric placement and relative directions shown in the figure
multi_step: solving requires determining the force direction on +Q from the -2Q charge and from the +3Q charge, then combining those effects
the phrase 'net direction of the electric force' implies vector addition rather than a single direct lookup
because the charges are different in sign and magnitude (+Q, -2Q, +3Q), the reasoning involves both attraction/repulsion and relative strength
verifiability: answer_text is a single discrete choice label ('b'), so final-answer matching is straightforward

registry_passed=true and an image file exists, but missing option text and image placeholder inconsistencies limit full semantic/process verification
